title: Add location toggle and user mode switching

requirements.summary: Add location enable/disable toggle to Profile page with localStorage persistence and integration with existing location permission system
acceptanceCriteria: Profile page shows a visible toggle switch labeled 'Location' that reflects the current enabled/disabled state.
Toggling off hides or disables location-dependent UI (e.g., map nearby buddies, Go Live button on MapPage).
The `locationEnabled` state persists across page reloads via localStorage.
When toggling on from a disabled state, the LocationPermissionModal is shown to re-request browser permission.
State defaults to `true` on first load if no localStorage value exists.

file_operations.description: Add `locationEnabled` state (boolean, defaults to true) and `setLocationEnabled` setter to SportContext. Initialize state from localStorage key 'sportbuddies_location_enabled' on mount. Sync changes back to localStorage whenever the state updates. Export both state and setter in the context value.

Add a toggle switch UI element labeled 'Location' that reads `locationEnabled` from SportContext and calls `setLocationEnabled` on toggle. When toggling from disabled to enabled, trigger the LocationPermissionModal to re-request browser permission using the existing `useLocationPermission` hook. Display the current state (enabled/disabled) visually. Wire the LocationPermissionModal component to appear when permission is needed.

Read `locationEnabled` from SportContext. When `locationEnabled` is false, hide or disable the 'Go Live' button and show a message indicating location is disabled. Optionally grey out or hide the nearby buddies list when location is disabled.

Read `locationEnabled` from SportContext. When `locationEnabled` is false and the user attempts to activate their sport presence, show a toast or alert prompting them to enable location in their profile settings before they can broadcast their presence.

Read `locationEnabled` from SportContext. When `locationEnabled` is false, disable the activate/deactivate presence buttons or show a message prompting the user to enable location in settings. Prevent activation attempts when location is disabled.

requirements.summary: Add userMode state to SportContext with persistence and mode selector UI in Profile page
acceptanceCriteria: SportContext exposes `userMode` (string) and `setUserMode` to all consumers.
Profile page renders a mode selector (segmented control or dropdown) showing at least 'Normal' and 'Buddy Finder' options.
Selecting a mode updates `userMode` in context and persists the value in localStorage.
The selected mode is restored from localStorage on app load.
Other pages that consume SportContext can read `userMode` to conditionally adjust behavior (e.g., MapPage highlights buddy-finder-specific UI when mode is `"buddy_finder"`).

file_operations.description: Add `userMode` state (string, defaults to 'normal') and `setUserMode` setter to SportContext. Initialize state from localStorage key 'sportbuddies_user_mode' on mount. Sync changes back to localStorage whenever the state updates. Define at least two mode values: 'normal' and 'buddy_finder'. Export both state and setter in the context value.

Add a mode selector UI component (segmented control or dropdown) that displays the current `userMode` from SportContext and allows switching between at least 'Normal' and 'Buddy Finder' modes. When the user selects a mode, call `setUserMode` with the new value. Label the control clearly as 'User Mode' or 'Mode' and show the active selection visually.

Read `userMode` from SportContext. When `userMode` is 'buddy_finder', apply visual highlighting or badging to the UI (e.g., highlight the map title, show a 'Buddy Finder Active' banner, or add a badge to nearby users). Provide a clear visual distinction that indicates the user is in buddy-finder mode.
